paper_title: Paleoceanographic study of K/Pg boundary at DSDP Site 465

methods.description: Method used to study the fragmentation of planktonic foraminifera to assess calcium carbonate dissolution, following the method described in Malmgren (1987).

steps.description: Usually more than 300 specimens per sample were counted to determine the proportion of fragments and its precision (95% confidence intervals).
steps.searchable_summary: Count planktonic foraminifera specimens per sample and determine the proportion of fragments and their 95% confidence intervals.
inputs: Prepared sediment samples containing planktonic foraminifera specimens
outputs: Total number of planktonic foraminifera counted per sample
Number of fragmented planktonic foraminifera per sample
Proportion of fragmented planktonic foraminifera per sample
95% confidence intervals for the proportion of fragments

methods.description: Method used to calculate the accumulation rate (number of specimens/kyr/cm2) to visualize changes in abundance of a specific species (P. globocameratu sp. nov.) across the K/Pg boundary. This metric approximates population density.

steps.description: The number of specimens/kyr/cm2 was calculated as D*N*S, where D is the density of the sediment (set to 1.925 g/cm3 in this study), N is the number of specimens per gram sediment and S is the sedimentation rate in cm/kyr as given in Table 3.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate accumulation rate using sediment density, specimen count per gram, and sedimentation rate.
inputs: Number of specimens per gram sediment (N) for the target species (P. globocameratu sp. nov.)
outputs: Accumulation rate (specimens/kyr/cm2) for the target species
